title: Fresh-agent onboarding test
short_description: Run and pass the fresh-agent test: an agent session with no prior context, given only the installed package and shipped docs, produces a verified multi-scene video without human debugging, using the Phase 2 harness pointed at onboarding.
long_description_md:
# Fresh-agent onboarding test

## Why this exists

Product-spec success metric 1 (the v1 bar) and capability 11 both hinge on one falsifiable claim: an agent with zero prior context on this project, given only `pip install anima-ai` and the shipped docs, can author and ship a verified video with no human debugging. Every other Phase 7 task (`agent-docs-surface`, `cookbook-examples`) is an input to this test, not a substitute for it — docs that look good to a human reviewer but fail a real cold agent session are not done.

## Roadmap fit

Phase 7, third done-when bullet, verbatim: "Fresh-agent test passes: an agent session with no prior context, given only the installed package + shipped docs, produces a verified multi-scene video without human debugging (the harness from Phase 2, pointed at onboarding instead of repair)." The Phase 2 harness referenced is the "agent-in-the-loop regression suite" done-when item — "a fresh agent session given only verify JSON fixes ≥9 [of 10 broken fixtures] without rendering — measured, in-repo harness, repeatable" — built by `agent-repair-harness`. This task does not rebuild that harness; it adds an onboarding *mode* to it (build-from-scratch instead of repair-a-broken-fixture) and runs it for real.

Depends on `agent-docs-surface` (the docs the fresh agent gets), `cookbook-examples` (the worked patterns it can read), `agent-repair-harness` (the measurement machinery this task repoints).

## Required scope

**Onboarding mode.** Extend the existing harness's target/config model with an "onboard" mode: instead of handing the agent a deliberately-broken fixture project to repair, hand it a task prompt ("produce a verified N-scene video on topic X using only the installed package and shipped docs") with zero other context — no prior transcript, no hints, no cookbook excerpt pasted into the prompt (the agent must find the cookbook itself via the docs surface). Config is data, testable without a live agent call.

**Repeatable scoring.** Trial outcome (pass/fail, whether the agent needed any human intervention, whether `verify` ultimately passed, how many verify iterations it took) must be a pure function computed from the recorded transcript + artifacts (verify JSON reports, render/preview outputs, agent action log) — not something that requires a second live agent call to re-score. Unit-testable against canned fixture trial records.

**The live run.** Execute the harness in onboarding mode for real: a genuinely fresh agent session (new context window, no seeded knowledge of this codebase beyond what `pip install anima-ai` + shipped docs give it) attempts the task end to end. Record transcript, all artifacts, and the harness's pass/fail verdict. This is a real, uncached LLM agent session — a live cost, however small against existing tooling budgets — so per the zero-budget persona contract, flag to Arnav before running a multi-trial batch. Test passes when the harness's own documented onboarding-mode threshold (mirroring the Phase 2 repair threshold's rigor) is met and logged.

## Out of scope

- Building the underlying harness's transcript-capture/scoring infrastructure from zero — that's `agent-repair-harness`; this task adds an onboarding target/mode to it.
- Writing the docs or cookbook the agent reads — `agent-docs-surface`, `cookbook-examples`.
- The 15-20 minute flagship episode itself — `v1-flagship-episode` is a distinct, larger, founder-judged bar; this test is multi-scene, not full-length.
- Fixing any doc/API gap this run surfaces beyond what's needed to pass — a systemic gap found here that needs deeper engine work is a new backlog item, not silently absorbed into this task's scope.
roadmap_phase: Phase 7: Agent onboarding surface + the v1 bar

deliverables.description: Onboarding-mode target/config added to the existing agent harness: zero-context task prompt (build N-scene verified video, no fixture/hints given), distinct from the Phase 2 repair-mode target. Unit tested for config parsing and mode selection.

Deterministic, pure-function scoring of a trial outcome (pass/fail, human-intervention flag, verify-iteration count) computed from recorded transcript + artifacts alone, no live re-scoring call. Unit tested against canned fixture trial records covering pass, fail-needed-human-help, and fail-verify-never-passed cases.

Live fresh-agent onboarding run: real agent session with no prior context, only installed package + shipped docs, attempts the task end to end; harness records transcript and artifacts and reports pass/fail against its documented threshold. Multi-trial batches cost real LLM usage — flag to Arnav first per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md zero-budget contract before running.

verification.steps: 1. Flag the planned trial batch (count + estimated cost) to Arnav per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md before running, since this is a real live-agent-session cost even against zero external spend threshold. 2. Run the onboarding harness for the configured trial count with a genuinely fresh agent context (no seeded transcript, no cookbook excerpt in the prompt). 3. Confirm each trial's recorded verify report shows `pass: true` and the transcript/action log shows zero human debugging intervention. 4. Confirm the harness's aggregate pass rate meets its documented onboarding threshold. 5. Log the run (trial count, pass rate, any doc/cookbook gaps the agent hit) in docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md.
